---
title: "Gendreau Lab Research Hub"
subtitle: "Neurosurgical outcomes research, predictive modeling, and web-based calculators"
toc: false
---

::: {.hero-banner}
## Translating clinical data into usable neurosurgical research tools

The Gendreau Lab develops neurosurgical outcomes research, predictive models, and web-based calculators using national registry and institutional data sources. This site serves as a central hub for the lab's publications, active projects, and deployed predictive tools.

[View calculators](calculators.qmd){.btn .btn-primary} [View publications](publications.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary} [Contact](contact.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-secondary}
:::

::: {.feature-grid}
::: {.feature-card}
### Predictive calculators

Interactive web-based tools are listed with links to their live Shiny/Posit deployments and associated manuscripts when available.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Publications

Peer-reviewed papers, abstracts, and selected manuscripts are organized by research area and updated as citations are finalized.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Research focus

Current work spans neuro-oncology, neurotrauma, registry-based epidemiology, clinical informatics, and web-based prediction tools.
:::
:::

## Featured calculator

::: {.tool-card .featured-tool}
### Intracranial Chordoma Survival Calculator

NCDB-based survival prediction tool for intracranial chordoma.

[Launch calculator](https://garingriffith-chordoma-survival-calculator.share.connect.posit.cloud/){.btn .btn-primary}
:::

## Research areas

- Neuro-oncology outcomes and survival prediction
- Traumatic brain injury prognosis and resource utilization
- Registry-based neurosurgical epidemiology
- Clinical informatics and model-assisted abstraction
- Web-based calculator development and deployment
- Vascular and perioperative outcomes research

## Research-use disclaimer

The calculators and materials on this site are intended for research and educational use. They are not medical devices and are not a substitute for professional medical judgment, multidisciplinary discussion, or individualized patient counseling.
